body:
tracker.py is still 689 lines after the fsck + remote splits (T-134 and follow-up 08e66e9). The next cleanly-extractable cluster is event-log handling: a self-contained group of ~80 lines that currently live directly on Tracker.

Scope: move these Tracker methods into a new EventLog class in scripts/gittoc_lib/event_log.py, composed into Tracker as self.events (same pattern as self.remote):

- event_path(issue_id, state)
- find_event_path(issue_id)
- move_event_file(issue_id, new_state, previous_path)
- append_event(issue, kind, text, actor)
- event_entries(issue_id)    # includes the JSONL parse + note_id numbering + per-instance cache
- filtered_events(issue_id, kinds, limit)
- note_count(issue_id)

The _event_cache dict moves onto EventLog. Call sites in commands.py, remote_sync.py (none today), integrity.py (uses event_entries + filtered_events via fsck's _validate_event_file), and tracker.py itself (create_issue, update_issue, add_note append events) need to be rewritten: tracker.event_entries(i) -> tracker.events.entries(i), tracker.append_event(...) -> tracker.events.append(...), etc.

Drop method-name prefixes ('event_' / '_event') now that the class namespaces them: path, find, move_file, append, entries, filtered, note_count.

Expected tracker.py size after: ~600 lines. Expected event_log.py size: ~100 lines.

Non-goals: no API-compat shims (self-contained project, no external callers). Do NOT leave thin wrappers on Tracker — rewrite call sites directly. integrity.py should import EventLog or receive an events object; decide based on whether fsck conceptually belongs to Tracker (keep tracker.events access) or stands alone (pass events in).

Acceptance: all 129+ tests still pass; tracker.py below ~650 lines; no method named event_* or _event_* remains on Tracker.
title: split event-log handling out of Tracker into an EventLog class
